summary: Patch sacred_texts.extract_text_page() to strip inline <font size=1> footnote refs and trailing Footnotes block
description:
## Why Quality improvement to `extract_text_page()`. **Not a blocker** for the Upanishads or pagan tickets — the pipeline tolerates the artifacts today and the existing Yasna corpus runs with them. Full rationale, code-path analysis, decision matrix: `docs/corpus-expansion/sbe-downloader-patch-assessment.md`. ## Problem Current `extract_text_page()` (scripts/downloaders/sacred_texts.py:138-204) only decomposes class-based footnote containers (`class_=["footnotes","notes","fn"]`). SBE-volume pages do not use those classes — they use bare `` for inline footnote-ref superscripts and a trailing `

Footnotes

` apparatus block. Empirical evidence (committed `raw/zoroastrianism/yasna-30.txt`): - Bare-digit ref glued mid-sentence: `"a worse 5 , as to thought, as to word, and as to deed"` - Bare-digit ref before period: `"(The qualifying words are all in the neuter 1 .)"` - Full Footnotes apparatus appended to translation: `25:1`, `26:1`, ... `35:6` notes See `sbe-downloader-patch-assessment.md` §3.1 (markup) and §3.2 (committed evidence). ## Patch scope 1. Decompose `` and `` elements before `main.get_text()`. 2. Drop the trailing apparatus block from `

Footnotes

` onward. 3. Verify zero false-positives across the corpus — the `font size="1"` selector is sacred-texts-SBE-specific, but spot-check Egyptian (Budge) and other non-SBE sacred-texts pages. ## Re-acquire + re-chunk + re-embed plan Already-shipped SBE sources need re-acquisition once the patch lands: - Zoroastrian: `gathas-introduction`, `yasna-28..51`, `yasna-53`, `bundahishn` (manifest.toml:375-440) - Upanishads (if ticket 1 shipped first): all `upanishads.*` entries - Future Buddhist SBE49/SBE10/SBE11 if added per `docs/corpus-expansion-candidates.md` §3.7, §3.8, §4.11 ## Follow-up Once the patch lands, revisit the bare `p. N` policy at `scripts/chunk.py:63-65`. The carve-out was because footnote-ref digits looked similar to page markers; that ambiguity goes away once refs are stripped. ## Files - `scripts/downloaders/sacred_texts.py` — patch target - `docs/corpus-expansion/sbe-downloader-patch-assessment.md` — full analysis - `sources/manifest.toml` — SBE sources to re-acquire

resolution.note: extract_text_page now decomposes font[size=1] + <sup> + the trailing Footnotes <h3> block. 2 new fixtures + 10 existing tests pass. Re-acquire of already-shipped SBE Zoroastrian sources is the open follow-up; per the assessment doc it lands after the new SBE ingests (c8073f1d Upanishads, parts of da3ca85a) so a single re-acquire pass covers all of them at once.
